summary: PR #2484 matched HSD_ByteCodeEval in sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c, with supporting signature updates in bytecode.h and robj.c. The diff cleaned several generated-looking shapes: fmodf was rewritten with explicit quotient control flow, nested temporary blocks were collapsed, a boolean ladder became a direct expression, and the HSD_ByteCodeEval prototype moved to f32/s32 typedefs. Bot report showed one new match for main/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode HSD_ByteCodeEval from 99.92% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Primary matched implementation for HSD_ByteCodeEval; changed local helper fmodf, local declarations, stack operand handling, atan2 special case formatting, and boolean expression shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bytecode.c hunks for fmodf and HSD_ByteCodeEval

key_files.role: Owning header prototype updated from float*/int to f32*/s32 for HSD_ByteCodeEval.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8* pc, float* args, int nb_args) -> float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8* pc, f32* args, s32 nb_args)

key_files.role: Local extern declaration for HSD_ByteCodeEval updated to match the recovered signature used by expEvaluate context.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: extern float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8*, float*, u32) -> extern float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8*, f32*, s32)

what_changed: Matched HSD_ByteCodeEval in src/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c according to PR body and bot report.

Rewrote static inline fmodf from a single ternary using float parameters to an f32 helper with divisor/dividend parameters, explicit long long quotient, early return when fabs(divisor) > fabs(dividend), and dividend - divisor * quotient result.
Updated HSD_ByteCodeEval signature in implementation and header from float* args/int nb_args to f32* args/s32 nb_args.
Updated robj.c extern declaration for HSD_ByteCodeEval from float*/u32 to f32*/s32.
Separated HSD_SList* stack and list declarations and changed some locals from u32/int to int/s32 as shown in the diff.
Removed an intermediate f1 assignment in the fmod opcode case and passed the current stack data expression directly to fmodf.
Collapsed nested blocks around the atan2 special case while preserving fabsf_bitwise(f0) == 0.0F handling and stack writeback.
Replaced a verbose temporary bool ladder for the 0x32 case with a direct boolean expression equivalent to logical XOR on zero/nonzero operands.
Author-reported verification included ninja -j8, clang-format pre-commit on touched files, and pre-commit on touched files.
Bot report recorded matched code improvement and one new match for HSD_ByteCodeEval.
smart_moves: Kept the header prototype and the robj.c extern declaration synchronized with the recovered HSD_ByteCodeEval implementation signature.
Converted generated-looking nested scopes and temporary boolean variables into ordinary C expressions while the bot report still showed the target function as matched.
Used platform typedefs f32 and s32 consistently for the recovered function interface rather than leaving mixed float/int/u32 declarations.
Limited the PR scope to bytecode.c plus directly related declarations, with no broad data or split churn in the provided diff.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a matched function, signature recovery should propagate to the owning header and directly affected extern declarations rather than leaving mismatched local prototypes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ByteCodeEval signature changed in bytecode.c and bytecode.h
raw_diff: robj.c extern declaration changed to f32*/s32

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated-looking boolean ladders can sometimes be replaced with a direct source-level boolean expression and still match; here the 0x32 case became a direct zero/nonzero XOR-like expression.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: 0x32 case bool c/val ladder replaced with (d0 == 0 && d1 != 0) || (d0 != 0 && d1 == 0)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Unnecessary nested blocks around simple control flow should be tested for cleanup during matching; this PR removed extra braces around the atan2 special case without losing the reported match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: case with fabsf_bitwise(f0) == 0.0F nested blocks collapsed

naming_conventions.convention: Use project typedefs f32 and s32 in recovered sysdolphin/baselib prototypes when the matched implementation supports those exact types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ByteCodeEval uses f32* args and s32 nb_args in bytecode.c/bytecode.h
raw_diff: robj.c extern uses f32* and s32

naming_conventions.convention: Avoid treating broad AI/Codex-driven renaming as automatically accepted; the only review-comment evidence is an author note that Codex had renamed something everywhere and cleanup was possible.
evidence_refs: review_comment: idk why codex insisted on renaming this everywhere. can clean up if preferred

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use an explicit local long long quotient in a float modulo helper to control MWCC codegen instead of a compact ternary expression.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fmodf rewritten with long long quotient and separate assignment

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Test removing unnecessary temporaries where stack data can be passed directly; the fmod opcode case removed f1 and called fmodf(f0, ((ByteCodeVal*) &stack->data)->f).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: f1 assignment removed before fmodf call

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct boolean expressions may produce the desired match where decompiler output used bool temporaries and staged condition flags.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: 0x32 bool c/val ladder replaced by direct expression

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Keep match verification narrow and tied to touched files; the PR body lists ninja and pre-commit commands for bytecode.c, bytecode.h, and robj.c, while the bot report identifies the matched unit and symbol.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.text: idk why codex insisted on renaming this everywhere. can clean up if preferred
review_feedback.interpretation: The PR evidence records an author comment expressing uncertainty about Codex-driven renaming and openness to cleanup; it does not prove reviewer acceptance or rejection of a naming change.

follow_up_queries: HSD_ByteCodeEval signature f32 s32 robj extern
Codex insisted on renaming this everywhere

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
human_pr_text: PR body and verification commands
raw_diff: complete 4544-byte diff for three changed files
text_corpus: bot report with one new match and HSD_ByteCodeEval 100.00%
review_comments: author comment on Codex renaming
notes: The slice includes full diff, changed-file metadata, PR body, bot report, and one review comment, enough to ground file changes and matching outcome.
The review comment is authored by the PR author and should not be treated as independent reviewer direction.
The PR body claims local verification commands were run; the bot report independently supports the specific HSD_ByteCodeEval match, but no full build logs are included.
No evidence in the slice explains the exact assembly reason for each local type/name/order change, so matching tactics are framed as observed tactics rather than universal rules.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate recovered function signatures to headers and direct extern users
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2484 matched HSD_ByteCodeEval and updated the implementation, bytecode.h prototype, and robj.c extern to the same f32*/s32 signature. This supports the existing truthful-header practice for matched bodies.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Replace generated boolean temp ladders with direct boolean expressions when verified
accepted_candidate_records.text: The 0x32 case in HSD_ByteCodeEval changed from staged bool c/val temporaries into a direct zero/nonzero expression, and the bot report recorded HSD_ByteCodeEval as matched.

source_update_candidates.title: HSD_ByteCodeEval location and direct declaration surfaces
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2484, HSD_ByteCodeEval was matched in src/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c, its owning prototype appeared in src/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.h, and a direct extern declaration was present in src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path fact for future searches touching HSD bytecode evaluation and robj expression evaluation.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the fmodf parameter names divisor/dividend as a global naming rule; the PR evidence only shows this local helper change.
Do not infer that Codex renaming was rejected by reviewers; the only evidence is the author's own comment offering cleanup if preferred.
Do not claim every changed local declaration was necessary for the match; the diff and bot report show the final matched state but not per-change necessity.
